mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals weakening labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand growth, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward returns turn negative when both GDPC1 and RSAFS show quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects deteriorating manufacturing output and supply-side weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive returns in over 60 percent of periods where INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average, or the signal shows no statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive during periods of CPI acceleration above 0.4 percent month-over-month, or the signal's Rank IC drops below zero over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals a tightening labor market and rising wage pressures, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or its predictive accuracy vanishes when controlling for prior market trends.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in new housing starts indicates weakening residential investment and reduced demand for construction materials and consumer durables, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in over 65 percent of months where housing starts decline by more than 5 percent, or the signal fails to predict returns outside of recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling consumer prices indicates improving supply efficiency and margin expansion, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces negative forward returns in the majority of triggered instances, or interaction effects are statistically insignificant in multivariate regression tests.
